title: Trump says Iran war could stop ‘soon’; claims US has little left to target
content: US President Trump declared the war with Iran could end soon, claiming significant damage to Tehran's military. He stated US forces are ahead of schedule, having destroyed Iranian mine-laying boats in the Strait of Hormuz. Despite his optimism, US and Israeli officials indicate preparations for further strikes continue, with Iran vowing to block oil shipments.

title: Counting cost of Trump's war on Iran: Is US fighting a billion-dollar-a-day conflict?
content: Operation Epic Fury, now in its twelfth day, is costing the US an estimated $1 billion to $2 billion daily. Is this massive military expenditure involving extensive deployments and advanced weaponry raising concerns in Washington?

title: RCB vs SRH to kick off IPL 2026 at Chinnaswamy on March 28 - Full schedule inside
content: IPL 2026 kicks off March 28 with Royal Challengers Bengaluru facing Sunrisers Hyderabad in a Bengaluru opener. The BCCI announced the first 20 days of the schedule, featuring 20 matches across 10 venues. Full schedule awaits state election dates.

title: Trump-Ambani deal: Make America Refine Again — with help from RIL
content: A landmark $300 billion energy deal sees India's Reliance Industries partnering with Donald Trump to build America's first new oil refinery in 50 years. This ambitious project, planned for Texas, leverages Reliance's world-class refining expertise to process American shale oil for export. The alliance, forged through years of dynastic diplomacy, offers mutual benefits amidst global energy and geopolitical shifts.

title: Amazon orders 90-day reset, what new policy means for engineers and other top leaders
content: Amazon is tightening its code review and deployment rules for engineers following recent major outages, some linked to AI tools. A 90-day safety reset mandates dual reviews for changes to critical systems. This move comes after incidents caused millions in lost orders, prompting a top-down audit of code management practices.

title: Opposition's no-trust motion against Speaker Om Birla defeated in Lok Sabha
content: Lok Sabha rejected a no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla by voice vote amid opposition protests demanding Home Minister Amit Shah's apology. Shah criticized the move as rare and regrettable, emphasizing the Speaker's neutrality and the House's adherence to its own rules, not party dictates. Protests continued, disrupting proceedings.

title: USCIS issues 'alert', shares details of the form it will reject starting April 1
content: USCIS has updated Form I-129, effective April 1, 2026, requiring employers to detail educational and experience requirements for H-1B visa applicants. This change aims to align with the Department of Labor's wage level assignments and verify consistency between the Labor Condition Application and the registration process, impacting the upcoming FY27 H1B cap season.

title: Chinnaswamy set to host RCB's IPL 2026 matches — but there's a big twist
content: The M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru will host IPL 2026 matches, but only after approval from a government-appointed expert committee. The committee will inspect the stadium on March 13, 2026, to assess its preparedness. The BCCI announced the first phase of the tournament, running from March 28 to April 12, with 20 matches across 10 venues.

title: Neena Gupta shares story of friend evicted by kids over property
content: Veteran star Neena Gupta has issued a compelling reminder to parents regarding the hasty transfer of property to their offspring. Drawing from a poignant personal story about a friend's heart-wrenching experience of being cut off after a gift of a house, she underscored that parental affection does not always equate to gratitude or care from children.

title: Report claims Google, Microsoft, Palantir, IBM, Nvidia and Oracle are ‘Iran’s new targets’
content: Amidst escalating Middle East tensions, Iran's state media has identified major US tech firms like Microsoft, Nvidia, and IBM as potential targets. This warning follows an incident where Amazon's UAE data center was struck, causing a fire. The move signals a potential expansion of conflict into cyber and infrastructure warfare, with facilities in Israel, Dubai, and Abu Dhabi named.

title: 'Gill can’t get T20I captaincy': Ex-India cricketer makes shocking remark
content: After India’s third T20 World Cup win under Suryakumar Yadav, former opener Aakash Chopra said Shubman Gill is unlikely to return to the T20I side or become captain. Despite leading India in Tests and ODIs, Gill’s poor T20I form saw him miss the World Cup. Chopra advised him to focus on longer formats, while Sanju Samson and Ishan Kishan excelled.

title: 'King of the world' statue of Trump & Epstein in Titanic pose appears near US Capitol
content: A provocative sculpture, "King of the World," featuring Donald Trump and Jeffrey Epstein in a Titanic pose, was installed on the National Mall.  The artwork, by activist group Secret Handshake, criticizes their alleged ties, accompanied by banners urging "Make America Safe Again." This installation highlights renewed scrutiny of Trump's past association with Epstein amid ongoing document releases.

title: Gaurav Kapur-Kritika Kamra wedding: Farhan Akhtar, Zaheer Khan, Virender Sehwag attend
content: Actress Kritika Kamra and television host Gaurav Kapur are celebrating their wedding in Mumbai with a private ceremony. Close friends and family, including celebrities like Farhan Akhtar, Zaheer Khan, and Sagarika Ghatke, were seen arriving to bless the couple. The intimate celebrations are a departure from a grand affair, focusing on close connections.

title: Guinness record holder visits New Seven Wonders in 5 days, shares travel cost tips
content: Sujoy Kumar Mitra has set a Guinness World Record for the fastest time to visit the New Seven Wonders of the World, completing the journey in just over five days. This remarkable feat, achieved through meticulous planning and leveraging airline miles, highlights his extensive travel experience and dedication to breaking records.

title: Moon samples, 350 kg rover, 100-day mission life: Isro chief outlines Chandrayaan-4, 5 plans
content: India's ISRO is planning ambitious future missions, including Chandrayaan-4 for lunar sample return and Chandrayaan-5 with a heavier, longer-lasting lander. Future endeavors also encompass Mars landing and Venus missions, alongside the Gaganyaan human spaceflight program aiming for astronaut launches within two years.

title: Inside Simone Biles’ $3 million Texas home with gym, pool, and dog room
content: Olympic gymnastics icon Simone Biles has recently moved into her dream home after years of planning and construction. The celebrated gymnast and her husband, the NFL star Jonathan Owens, recently settled into their stunning waterfront mansion in Texas, a property reportedly worth around $3 million. The couple spent several years designing and building the house. The project finally has now been completed, allowing them to enjoy the home they always wanted.

title: Shah Rukh Khan features again on Hurun Global Rich List along with Taylor Swift, Rihanna, Jay Z
content: Bollywood superstar Shah Rukh Khan has once again made it to the Hurun Global Rich List with a net worth of USD 1.3 billion, solidifying his status as a major business figure. While his wealth saw a slight dip from last year, he remains among the world's wealthiest actors. Film producer Namit Malhotra also debuted on the list.

title: Khushboo Sundar on World Cup trophy being taken to temple: 'The trophy is for India'
content: Actor and politician Khushboo Sundar emphasized that the World Cup trophy belongs to all of India, urging people to view the win as national pride, not a religious matter. She defended taking the trophy to places of worship, citing cricketers' personal expressions of gratitude and devotion. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 1st ODI: Pakistan humbled in Bangladesh; hosts chase target in 15.1 overs
content: Bangladesh crushed Pakistan by eight wickets in the first ODI to take a 1-0 series lead. Pakistan collapsed to 114 after a strong start, with Nahid Rana taking five wickets and Mehidy Hasan Miraz three. In reply, Tanzid Hasan Tamim smashed 67 not out, helping Bangladesh chase the target comfortably in 15.1 overs. Pakistan’s bowlers struggled to create pressure.

title: Japan announces release of strategic oil reserves as Middle East tensions push prices up
content: Japan is releasing significant portions of its oil reserves to stabilize energy markets amidst escalating Middle East tensions. This strategic move, involving both private and state holdings, aims to counter supply concerns and calm global prices. The decision aligns with calls from the International Energy Agency for coordinated action among major economies to mitigate potential shipping disruptions.

title: Big revelation! 'MS Dhoni never said drop Yuvraj Singh': Former selector
content: Former chief selector Sandeep Patil has refuted claims by Yograj Singh that MS Dhoni was responsible for Yuvraj Singh's ouster from the Indian team. Patil stated on record that Dhoni never requested Yuvraj's exclusion and fully trusted the selection committee's decisions. He acknowledged a father's emotions but emphasised the blame was misplaced.

title: Kejriwal appeals to Delhi HC Chief Justice to transfer excise policy case to 'impartial' bench
content: Arvind Kejriwal and other accused in the excise policy case have requested the Delhi High Court Chief Justice to transfer their criminal revision petition from Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma's bench. They allege bias and express concerns over the bench's prima facie view and interim directions without hearing all parties.

title: Why Shivam Dube had to travel by train after India's T20 World Cup glory
content: After India’s 2026 T20 World Cup win, Shivam Dube couldn’t find flights from Ahmedabad to Mumbai, so he travelled by train with his wife and a friend in 3rd AC. To avoid being recognised, he wore a cap and mask and boarded early morning. A ticket checker almost exposed him, but his wife quickly dismissed the suspicion, and the journey stayed smooth.

title: 'Legitimate targets': Iran warns US tech firms including Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia
content: Iran has warned US tech companies that their offices and infrastructure could become targets as regional conflict escalates. The IRGC-affiliated Tasnim News Agency published a list of locations, including cloud services from Google and Microsoft, allegedly used for military purposes. Iran also threatened to target economic centers and banks linked to US and Israeli entities

title: ‘A welcome step’: Opposition backs TMC’s move to bring impeachment motion against CEC
content: Opposition parties, led by the Trinamool Congress, are planning an impeachment motion against the Chief Election Commissioner over alleged irregularities in West Bengal's electoral roll revision. Citing concerns over a large number of voters under scrutiny, parties like Congress and Samajwadi Party have voiced support.

title: Naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz? How these cheap but deadly underwater weapons work
content: Concerns are mounting over the Strait of Hormuz as US officials report Iran may be preparing to deploy naval mines. While initial deployments are limited, Iran possesses a substantial mine arsenal, prompting US strikes on Iranian vessels. The strait's critical role in global oil transport makes it vulnerable to such threats.

title: AI Express flight from Hyd makes a hard landing at Phuket, gets stuck on runway; all safe
content: An Air India Express Boeing 737 MAX experienced a hard landing in Phuket, with its nose wheels detaching upon the second touchdown. The aircraft, carrying 138 people, made a bounce after the initial impact. Fortunately, no injuries were reported, and the Directorate General of Civil Aviation is investigating the incident.

title: After T20 WC debacle, Pakistan crumble like a pack of cards vs B'desh
content: Pakistan were bowled out for 114 in 30.4 overs in the first ODI against Bangladesh after starting well with a 41-run opening stand. A sudden middle-order collapse saw wickets tumble quickly. Sahibzada Farhan (27) and Faheem Ashraf (37) were the only notable contributors. Nahid Rana starred with 5/24, while Mehidy Hasan Miraz took 3/29, leaving Bangladesh a modest chase.

title: Hansika Motwani divorces Sohael Khaturiya, no alimony sought
content: Actress Hansika Motwani and businessman Sohael Khaturiya have officially divorced, with the Bandra family court granting their mutual consent petition. The couple, married in December 2022, cited fundamental differences and irreconcilable disagreements as reasons for their separation. They have been living apart since July 2, 2024, and have made no financial or legal claims against each other.

title: Nifty50 closes at 23,866; BSE Sensex tanks 1,342 points - top reasons for fall
content: Stock market crash today: Continuing geopolitical tensions weighed on Nifty50 and BSE Sensex on Wednesday with the indices tanking over 1%. While Nifty50 went below the 24,000 mark, BSE Sensex crashed over 1,000 points.

title: 'I thought my dream was broken': Sanju Samson's emotional confession after T20 World Cup glory
content: Sanju Samson, India's leading run-scorer in the T20 World Cup, expressed profound gratitude for realizing a long-held dream.  Despite initial doubts after a tough series, Samson delivered crucial performances, earning Player of the Tournament. He emphasized fan prayers as more significant than personal accolades, highlighting the unexpected yet fulfilling nature of his success.

title: 'Dare you to score 30% in English': SC junks AI-drafted PIL, schools Ludhiana cloth trader
content: The Supreme Court dismissed a trader's PIL after he admitted using AI tools to draft it and couldn't explain legal terms. Chief Justice Surya Kant questioned the Ludhiana-based hosiery trader's understanding, even offering an "English exam.

title: Man diagnosed with cancer during office health checkup asked to resign
content: A senior manager diagnosed with thyroid cancer was terminated by his employer shortly after his diagnosis. Despite a strong performance record, he was given vague reasons for his dismissal. His subsequent hunger strike highlighted a critical gap in Indian labor laws regarding medical discrimination, prompting his fight for legislative reform.

title: Thank You, Surya! Now India must build for 2028
content: After leading India to the 2026 T20 World Cup, Suryakumar Yadav has set his sights on an Olympic gold in 2028 and a hat-trick of titles. Yet, despite an outstanding captaincy record, questions remain over his form, fitness and longevity. As India look ahead to the Olympics and the next World Cup, it may be time to start building the future under a new leader.

title: Priyanka Chopra on move to Hollywood: ‘I don’t like living in ...
content: Priyanka Chopra Jonas stepped away from Bollywood to pursue Hollywood opportunities. She felt creatively limited in India and sought wider representation for Indian actors.  Her journey was a risk, but she relied on her talent and work ethic.  She aimed to expand her horizons and explore new possibilities in the global entertainment industry.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: Players can officially sign at 4 p.m. ET Wednesday. We're following all NFL free agency moves as they happen.

title: Barnwell makes sense of Maxx Crosby trade, Ravens' ensuing Trey Hendrickson signing
content: Maxx Crosby was traded to the Ravens, the trade was called off and Baltimore signed Trey Hendrickson.

title: Colts, QB Jones agree to 2-year, $88M contract
content: The Colts and quarterback Daniel Jones reached agreement Wednesday on a two-year, $88 million contract that can be worth up to $100 million, his agents told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Sources: Ravens pivot, agree to add Hendrickson
content: The Ravens have agreed to sign Trey Hendrickson, sources told ESPN, in a high-priced pivot after their canceled blockbuster trade for Maxx Crosby.

title: Sports minister: Iran can't compete at World Cup
content: Iran cannot participate in the 2026 World Cup following airstrikes against the country by the United States and Israel, the Iranian sports minister said Wednesday.

title: Rolapp envisions ambitious changes for PGA Tour
content: CEO Brian Rolapp outlined a bold future for the PGA Tour on Wednesday, listing the possibility of relegation, a revamped schedule and match play in the playoffs among the changes being considered.

title: U.S. in jeopardy at WBC after upset loss to Italy
content: Italy stunned Team USA with an 8-6 victory in World Baseball Classic pool play Tuesday night, one of the biggest upsets in the tournament's 20-year history that puts the Americans in jeopardy of not advancing to the knockout round.

title: Syracuse fires Autry after 3 tourney-less years
content: Syracuse has fired men's basketball coach Adrian Autry after the Orange did not make the NCAA tournament in his three seasons at the helm.

title: Sources: Ravens rework Lamar deal for cap space
content: The Ravens have restructured quarterback Lamar Jackson's contract to create nearly $40 million in salary cap space, a source told ESPN.

title: Source: NFL exploring Thanksgiving Eve game
content: The NFL is exploring the possibility of playing a game the day before Thanksgiving, a source told ESPN.

title: 🏆 NFL free agency awards: Best and worst moves
content: The edge rush market exploded, the Titans called on old friends, Mike Evans landed in a new place, and two players were very particular about their contract numbers.

content: The United States' WBC fate is now out of its hands. Here's how the U.S. can move on -- or be sent packing.

title: All the records Bam Adebayo set in his stunning 83-point(!) night
content: Heat big man Bam Adebayo just put up the second-highest scoring game in NBA history, with 83 points in a win over the Wizards.

title: Avalanche now unbeatable? East contenders whiffed big-time? Judging NHL trade deadline overreactions
content: The final week resulted in a last flurry of trades -- and many hot takes. We identify which takeaways are legit and which are way off base.

title: Playoff watch: Projecting the Eastern wild-card teams
content: Montreal, Ottawa, Washington and Philadelphia will play on Wednesday. Here are playoff projections and more.

title: Avs critical of MacKinnon ejection for goalie injury
content: Colorado disagreed with the officials' decision to give star center Nathan MacKinnon a game misconduct.

title: Tottenham's unprecedented Champions League implosion felt like rock bottom
content: Not even the Metropolitano faithful knew what to make of Atlético's stunning win, with Spurs making numerous critical errors.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: Talks in 'right direction' after marathon meeting
content: League personnel and WNBPA staff met for nearly 12 hours Tuesday into Wednesday but did not reach a deal on a new collective bargaining agreement.

title: Latest mini NFL mock draft: Yates projects picks 1-10 based on the free agency frenzy
content: How has NFL free agency affected 2026 draft plans? Here are Field Yates' updated predictions for picks Nos. 1-10 after the first wave of deals.

title: Sources: Pats land Packers' Doubs on 4-year deal
content: The Patriots have agreed to sign former Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal worth up to $80 million, sources told ESPN.

title: Sources: TE Knox stays with Bills on new deal
content: The Bills found a way to keep tight end Dawson Knox on the 2026 roster amid a difficult cap situation by signing him to a new three-year contract, sources told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler on Tuesday.

title: Texans bolster O-line, reach deal with OT Smith
content: Free agent offensive tackle Braden Smith reached agreement with the Houston Texans on a two-year, $25 million contract that includes $13.5 million fully guaranteed, his agent told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Source: WR Raymond leaves Lions to join Bears
content: Kalif Raymond has agreed to sign a one-year contract with the Bears, reuniting the wide receiver with Ben Johnson, a source told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

title: Source: LB Lloyd gets $45M deal with Panthers
content: Former Jaguars linebacker Devin Lloyd agreed to a three-year, $45 million deal with the Panthers, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Sources: Tua to sign 1-year deal with Falcons
content: Former Miami Dolphins quarterback Tua Tagovailoa plans to sign a one-year deal with the Atlanta Falcons, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Commanders land Oweh on 4-year, $100M deal
content: Former Chargers defensive end Odafe Oweh, who has 17.5 sacks since 2024, has agreed with the Commanders on a four-year, $100 million deal, his agents told ESPN.

title: Sources: QB Willis, Fins reach 3-year, $67.5M deal
content: Free agent QB Malik Willis, after reviving his career with the Packers, has agreed to a three-year, $67.5 million deal with the Dolphins, sources told ESPN.

title: NWSL ambition rankings: Which teams are raising standards, and which aren't trying enough?
content: The NWSL has teams that are building stadiums, signing stars and spending money. Meanwhile, other teams, well, aren't doing any of that.

title: Is 23 red cards in Brazilian mass brawl a world record? Not even close
content: A lot of red cards were handed out in Cruzeiro's win over Atlético-MG, but it's nowhere near the world record.

content: From Scottie Scheffler's attempt at a third Players title to how the course is set up to play, we have five big topics to keep an eye on this week.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Bernardo Silva could join Messi at Inter Miami
content: Manchester City's Bernardo Silva could be joining Lionel Messi at Inter Miami. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: LPG crisis fuels fear of loss of livelihood, food scarcity in Kerala
content: Slackening availability of commercial LPG cylinders has pushed small-scale entrepreneurs to the brink in Kerala, with many facing indefinite closure

title: Interest of the state to preserve life cannot overpower right to dignified death: Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court upheld the withdrawal of life support to a 32-year-old man in a persistent vegetative state for over 12 years

title: Fire destroys plastic pipes in Amaravati
content: Home Minister Vangalapudi Anitha orders an inquiry to determine wether it was an accident or a conspiracy

title: Civilians held captive, fresh tension in Manipur’s Ukhrul
content: Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh directs senior police and administrative officials to secure the release of the people who were travelling on the Ukhrul-Imphal Road

title: Mamata holds review meeting on LPG crisis, calls for SOP and rationing of cylinders
content: People were seen crowding the offices of distribution agencies. Many complained that they have run out of LPG and cannot book a refill. Some schools said that they have no LPG to cook mid-day meals

title: Chief Minister to inaugurate Pallivasal extension scheme on Thursday
content: He will also officially inaugurate the Golden Jubilee celebrations of the Idukki Hydroelectric Project, the State’s largest power project

title: Notice on ‘operational limitations’ put up inside Government Medical College Hospital Pathanamthitta causes embarrassment
content: The notice posted on the walls at the Surgery wing of the hospital stated that patients coming for surgery should be aware of the hospital’s limitations

title: BKTC proposes ban on ‘non-Sanatanis’ at Badrinath, Kedarnath
content: Anyone who has faith in Sanatan Dharma is a Sanatani; anyone who applies vermillion on the forehead and can recite the Chalisa is a Sanatan, says BKTC chairman

content: It comes amid growing concern over the State govt.’s proposal to establish Karnataka Public Schools by closing down government schools

title: SVIMS ‘Pink Bus’ screens 42,000 rural women for cancer
content: Screening camp organised at the Community Health Centre at Naravaripalle, the Chief Minister’s ancestral village

title: Ongole GGH holds mock drill to test mass casualty response
content: Hospital staff simulate road accident scenario and activate Orange Alert to assess rapid response and coordination with 108 ambulance services

title: Tobacco auctions halted again as farmers protest sharp fall in prices
content: Apart from the tax burden, sources in the tobacco industry say geopolitical tensions could also be affecting procurement

title: Kadapa MP seeks new railway line to boost local economy
content: Avinash Reddy urges Railways to act on Andhra Pradesh’s proposal to link Pulivendula, Muddanur and Puttaparthi, citing connectivity to cement plants and the Kopparthi industrial estate

title: Andhra Pradesh explores Artificial Intelligence-based cultural tourism initiatives
content: Tourism Department and Eros Innovation decide to work together to explore technological partnerships that connect culture, digital innovation, and tourism

title: ESIC Medical College celebrates foundation day, women’s day
content: Focus on various initiatives, including activities aimed at strengthening healthcare delivery, raising awareness and encouraging staff engagement

title: 10 Maoists surrender in Odisha
content: The cadres who surrendered arms and ammunition were from Chhattisgarh and had a combined reward of ₹1.15 crore on their head

title: Solar conspiracy case: court dismisses Ganesh Kumar’s plea to stay proceedings
content: The defense argued that the ongoing witness examinations were receiving excessive media attention and were being politically exploited by opponents to gain an unfair electoral advantage

title: TUDA begins Avilala tank restoration, plans revival of 22 water bodies
content: It clears 187 acres on tank bed and seeks public opinion as part of Tirupati’s City of Tanks and Lakes initiative

title: Despite hurdles by YSRCP, development will not stop, asserts HRD Minister Lokesh
content: Referring to a social media post stating that a PIL has been filed in the High Court challenging land allotment for Google in Vizag, the Minister questions why YSRCP president Jagan Mohan Reddy harbours hatred for Andhra Pradesh’s youth and development

title: Panel estimates Lakkundi treasure to be at least 500 years old
content: Going by the rate of gold, the value of the ornaments is put at ₹80 lakh but the value is at least 10 times more, as they are ancient

content: Congress leader says “vile things” were being said about him during the ongoing debate; alleges he has been stopped from speaking multiple times, including when raising questions about the PM’s “compromises”

title: Students should not depend excessively on AI for research: KSOU V-C

content: Company plans to establish a state-of-the-art 17.80 MTPA Integrated Steel Plant in two phases at Rajayyapeta in Anakapalli district, and the first phase will have a production capacity of 8.20 MTPA; it invites Expression of Interest for establishment of a dedicated 1-million-tonne steel fabrication unit

title: Vontimitta gears up for grand Sita Rama Kalyanam on April 1

title: When Supreme Court First Recognised Concept Of Passive Euthanasia

content: West Asia's turmoil is triggering global anxiety across commodities, and it's not just oil, but fertilisers, chemicals, feedstocks, and energy-intensive materials.

content: A van slammed into a security barricade near the White House early Wednesday, police said, triggering a full lockdown of downtown Washington.

title: "Shashi Tharoor Should Counsel Them": Amit Shah's Swipe At Opposition
content: Amit Shah slammed the Opposition for bringing a notice seeking the removal of Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and also took a swipe at the Congress and the Leader of the Opposition (LoP) for allegedly stoking pandemonium in the House with false claims.

title: Mumbai's Iconic Hotels Forced to Dim Their Lights As LPG Dry Spell Continues
content: The "City That Never Sleeps" is waking up to a silent crisis not of demand, but of fuel. Mumbai's iconic hospitality sector is currently gasping for air as a severe shortage of commercial LPG cylinders has brought the industry to its knees.

title: "Truly Court Of People Of India": Ex-Chief Justice On Passive Euthanasia Order
content: Calling it a compassionate exercise of constitutional power, former chief justice of India DY Chandrachud praised the Supreme Court on Wednesday after it delivered its first-ever order allowing passive euthanasia.

title: The Challenges In Reopening Strait Of Hormuz, A Vital Oil Chokepoint

title: Opposition's No-Confidence Motion Against Speaker Defeated In Lok Sabha
content: The Opposition's no-confidence motion against Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla ended, as expected, in a defeat -- but not before considerable fireworks in the lower house today.

title: "Mobiles Not Luxury": Raghav Chadha Questions 28-Day "Monthly" Recharges
content: Millions of prepaid mobile users risk being cut off in emergencies due to restrictive recharge rules, Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha said in Parliament on Wednesday.

title: 2 Indians Dead, 1 Missing In Middle East War, Confirms Foreign Ministry
content: The spokesperson of the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), Randhir Jasiwal, on Wednesday confirmed that the two Indian nationals have lost their lives and one still remains missing amid the ongoing Middle Eastern conflict.

content: Amid escalating Israel-Iran tensions, around 100 Indian students stranded in Iran are expected to return to India on March 14 and 15 via commercial flights after travelling to Armenia by road.

title: "Questioning Speaker's Integrity Means Questioning Our Democracy": Amit Shah
